chunks.doc_name: A review of three recent FDA warning letters to medical device manufacturers reveals a few similarities in quality system deficiencies, highlighting systemic issues across different device… | The FDA Group
chunks.text: # The FDA Group’s Post [The FDA Group](https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-fda-group?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 18,071 followers * [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fthe-fda-group_a-review-of-three-recent-fda-warning-letters-activity-7262597679078125568-o12R&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) A review of three recent FDA warning letters to medical device manufacturers reveals a few similarities in quality system deficiencies, highlighting systemic issues across different device categories. We've pulled out the common problems the FDA is finding and citing. Read the full warning letters: 1: [https://lnkd.in/gHqc2p3T](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FgHqc2p3T&urlhash=4Der&trk=public_post-text) 2: [https://lnkd.in/g64qE\_KA](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Fg64qE_KA&urlhash=ScCl&trk=public_post-text) 3: [https://lnkd.in/g7tQz65c](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Fg7tQz65c&urlhash=rRFY&trk=public_post-text) 🚩 Design Control System Failures Arguably the most significant shared deficiency centers on fundamental design control failures. In one case, a manufacturer of sterile convenience kits failed to validate 25 different FDA product codes, including critical surgical kits where a medium-risk cautery component and smoke evacuation device had never been verified for ethylene oxide sterilization compatibility. Two X-ray system manufacturers demonstrated similar oversights — one shipped a Class II portable system without any design validation or risk analysis documentation since 2023, while another failed to validate its imaging panel integration and control software, critical components that directly affect diagnostic image quality and system performance. 🚩 Documentation and Records Documentation failures were pervasive and specific. One manufacturer couldn't provide DMFs for any of their 25 product codes, leaving no trail of design decisions or component verification. Another lacked basic assembly and manufacturing documentation, with no records showing whether technicians completed critical production steps or followed required procedures. The third inappropriately documented significant design changes as temporary manufacturing deviations, bypassing crucial verification requirements for changes affecting product fit, form, and function. 🚩 CAPA System Inadequacies CAPA systems showed critical weaknesses. In one case, 19 CAPAs implemented between 2022 and 2024 had no verification of effectiveness and no documented evidence of implementation. Another manufacturer's CAPA system failed to employ any statistical methodology to detect recurring quality problems, with no trending of device failures or analysis of quality data patterns. Field issues, including software failures affecting device operation and humidity-related generator malfunctions, weren't properly investigated or corrected. —— Want to stay out of our warning letter breakdowns? Contact us to access our global network of 2,500+ consultants, 225+ former FDA. We run audits, mock inspections, and remediation for 17 of the top 25 life science firms. [Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fthe-fda-group_a-review-of-three-recent-fda-warning-letters-activity-7262597679078125568-o12R&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fthe-fda-group_a-review-of-three-recent-fda-warning-letters-activity-7262597679078125568-o12R&trk=public_post_comment-cta) To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fthe-fda-group_a-review-of-three-recent-fda-warning-letters-activity-7262597679078125568-o12R&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta) ## More Relevant Posts * [Eureka](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/eureka?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 4,480 followers + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Feureka_intertronics-highlights-adhesive-process-activity-7379460948320808960-xgnp&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) [Intertronics](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/intertronics?trk=public_post-text), a specialist in medical device adhesives, has published a new white paper titled Process Validation in Medical Device Bonding: Controlling Variables. Read more here: [https://lnkd.in/eh96ZZ7E](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Feh96ZZ7E&urlhash=2IUD&trk=public_post-text) [#MedtechManufacturing](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmedtechmanufacturing&trk=public_post-text) [#ProcessValidation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fprocessvalidation&trk=public_post-text) [#AdhesiveBonding](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fadhesivebonding&trk=public_post-text) [#EurekaMagazine](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Feurekamagazine&trk=public_post-text) [Intertronics Highlights Adhesive Process Validation in Medical Devices - Eureka eurekamagazine.co.uk](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Eeurekamagazine%2Eco%2Euk%2Fcontent%2Fnews%2Fintertronics-highlights-adhesive-process-validation-in-medical-devices%2F&urlhash=TWeW&trk=public_post_feed-article-content) [5](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Feureka_intertronics-highlights-adhesive-process-activity-7379460948320808960-xgnp&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Feureka_intertronics-highlights-adhesive-process-activity-7379460948320808960-xgnp&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Feureka_intertronics-highlights-adhesive-process-activity-7379460948320808960-xgnp&trk=public_post_comment-cta) To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Feureka_intertronics-highlights-adhesive-process-activity-7379460948320808960-xgnp&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta) * [Intec Automation, Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/intec-automation-inc-?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 1,697 followers + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fintec-automation-inc-_equipment-to-automate-low-mix-high-volume-activity-7384622920821768193-U0oz&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) Medical device manufacturers face a unique challenge when producing consumables such as diagnostic cartridges, IV catheters, and surgical components. These assemblies often fall into the “low mix, high volume” category or limited product variation but massive daily output. Meeting that demand with manual processes stretches labor, risks quality, and drives up costs, especially under strict regulatory oversight. Automation provides the consistency and throughput these assemblies require. [#medicaldevices](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmedicaldevices&trk=public_post-text) [#assembly](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fassembly&trk=public_post-text) [#manufacturing](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmanufacturing&trk=public_post-text) Learn more: [https://hubs.la/Q03NYq560](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fhubs%2Ela%2FQ03NYq560&urlhash=lhMJ&trk=public_post-text)

compliance of medical devices to third-party service providers. This approach helps manufacturers reduce costs, accelerate time-to-market, and focus on core competencies while ensuring quality and compliance with global regulatory standards. * [Samantha Deverell](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/samanthadeverell?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamanthadeverell_how-heat-shrink-tubing-enhances-medical-device-activity-7379402278694338560-za8C&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) One thing I’ve noticed in medical device manufacturing is how unforgiving it can be. There’s no room for inconsistency, not when you’re working on something as critical as a catheter or a diagnostic probe. Heat shrink tubing is often seen as just another component, but in reality, it does a lot of heavy lifting: insulation, protection, and finishing on some very complex assemblies. The interesting part is how differently materials behave once you put them under real-world pressures such as sterile environments, regulatory demands and production deadlines. That’s where I think FEP heat shrink tends to stand out. The clarity makes inspection easier, it doesn’t flinch at sterilisation, and it has this reassuring consistency over long lengths. Those small details matter because they save you from the headaches of rework and quality issues later on. It’s about making the whole manufacturing process that little bit smoother. [https://vist.ly/48yr2](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fvist%2Ely%2F48yr2&urlhash=4Y9e&trk=public_post-text) [How Heat Shrink Tubing Enhances Medical Device Manufacturing adtech.co.uk](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fadtech%2Eco%2Euk%2Fblog%2Fhow-heat-shrink-tubing-enhances-medical-device-manufacturing&urlhash=iaV9&trk=public_post_feed-article-content) [3](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamanthadeverell_how-heat-shrink-tubing-enhances-medical-device-activity-7379402278694338560-za8C&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamanthadeverell_how-heat-shrink-tubing-enhances-medical-device-activity-7379402278694338560-za8C&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamanthadeverell_how-heat-shrink-tubing-enhances-medical-device-activity-7379402278694338560-za8C&trk=public_post_comment-cta) To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamanthadeverell_how-heat-shrink-tubing-enhances-medical-device-activity-7379402278694338560-za8C&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta) * [Dan Sevcenco 🌍📈🎯](https://es.linkedin.com/in/dansevcenco?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdansevcenco_quality-medicaldevices-patientsafety-activity-7379047437866401793-pFOm&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) Quality in Medical Devices: Why It Matters! ⏳Quality is critical in medical device manufacturing for patient safety and product success. Here’s why: 1. Protects for patient safety; 2. Maintains reliability and effectiveness; 3. Builds manufacturer trust and reputation; 4. Saves costs by preventing recalls and rework; 5. Enables traceability for issue identification and recalls; 6. Ensures compliance tight with ISO 9001, ISO 13485, CE & FDA, and CAPA processes. 💡Example Scenario: A hospital reports a device (e.g., pump or dock station) malfunction, raising a safety or compliance issue. Steps to resolve: 🔎1. Gather Information Promptly: a. Log files (if exportable); b. Detailed problem description; c. Request device to be sent to manufacturer’s office or HQ; d. Photos of device condition, serial number, settings, and consumables (LOT numbers); e. If the device can’t be sent or multiple issues are reported, visit the distributor and end-user. 📋2. Investigate at

chunks.doc_name: A review of three recent FDA warning letters to medical device manufacturers reveals a few similarities in quality system deficiencies, highlighting systemic issues across different device… | The FDA Group
chunks.text: a safety or compliance issue. Steps to resolve: 🔎1. Gather Information Promptly: a. Log files (if exportable); b. Detailed problem description; c. Request device to be sent to manufacturer’s office or HQ; d. Photos of device condition, serial number, settings, and consumables (LOT numbers); e. If the device can’t be sent or multiple issues are reported, visit the distributor and end-user. 📋2. Investigate at Facility: a. Inspect all modules; b. Reproduce the issue; c. Identify root cause (device or human error); d. Compare with similar devices and consumables; e. Use tools (e.g., multimeter) to check voltage, resistance, or temperature. 📈 3. Propose Solutions (Based on Results and final decisions, ex): a. Replace consumable batches or/and offer discounts; b. For widespread issues, recall all affected products globally; c. Offer free repairs, spare parts, bonuses, or extended warranties; d. Recall and replace affected devices or/and discount for new models. 📌NOTE! Manufacturers must maintain close contact with distributors and end-users to resolve issues quickly and implement robust CAPA processes to prevent recurrence. 📷P.S. The attached image shows a real compliance issue! What’s your take on quality in medical devices? Share below! * [Neki Sharma](https://in.linkedin.com/in/neki-sharma-31b954204?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fneki-sharma-31b954204_understanding-medical-device-classification-activity-7386741206837485569-uZde&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) 🔬 Understanding Medical Device Classification under ISO 13485 ISO 13485 defines the Quality Management System (QMS) framework for the design, production, and distribution of medical devices — ensuring safety, consistency, and regulatory compliance. Medical devices are classified based on risk to the patient and user, and each class follows specific regulatory affairs guidelines under ISO 13485 and India’s IMDR 2017 (CDSCO) framework. --- 🩺 Types of Medical Devices Class Risk Level Examples Class A Low risk Thermometers, bandages, stethoscopes Class B Low to moderate risk Suction equipment, dental materials, infusion sets Class C Moderate to high risk Dialysis machines, ultrasound scanners, infusion pumps Class D High risk Cardiac stents, pacemakers, ventilators --- ⚖️ Regulatory Affairs Guidelines (ISO 13485 + IMDR 2017) Aspect Class A Class B Class C Class D Licensing Authority - State Licensing Authority (SLA) SLA Central Licensing Authority (CLA) CLA Quality System (QMS) - ISO 13485 ISO 13485 Full ISO 13485 + site audit Full ISO 13485 + site audit Documentation - Basic product details, label Design dossier + risk analysis Complete design dossier + validation Full technical file + clinical data Clinical Evidence - Not required May be required Required Mandatory Manufacturing License - Form MD-5 Form MD-5 Form MD-9 Form MD-9 Audit Frequency - Every 5 years Every 3 years Every 2 years Annual / as required --- 🌍 Global Alignment Regulation Equivalent Mapping EU MDR 2017/745 Class I → A, IIa → B, IIb → C, III → D US FDA (21 CFR Part 820) Class I → A, Class II → B/C, Class III → D --- 💡 Key Insight: ISO 13485 ensures that every medical device — from a simple thermometer to a complex cardiac stent — meets rigorous standards for quality, safety, and performance. Higher-risk devices require more robust regulatory oversight, clinical

Higher-risk devices require more robust regulatory oversight, clinical validation, and continuous post-market monitoring. --- [160](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fneki-sharma-31b954204_understanding-medical-device-classification-activity-7386741206837485569-uZde&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [2 Comments](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fneki-sharma-31b954204_understanding-medical-device-classification-activity-7386741206837485569-uZde&trk=public_post_social-actions-comments) [Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fneki-sharma-31b954204_understanding-medical-device-classification-activity-7386741206837485569-uZde&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fneki-sharma-31b954204_understanding-medical-device-classification-activity-7386741206837485569-uZde&trk=public_post_comment-cta) To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fneki-sharma-31b954204_understanding-medical-device-classification-activity-7386741206837485569-uZde&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta) * [TTP plc](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/ttp-product-and-technology-consultancy?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 20,771 followers + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fttp-product-and-technology-consultancy_ttp-achieves-iso-13485-certification-for-activity-7385968902851428352-hbjU&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) TTP achieves ISO 13485 certification for manufacturing medical devices, implantables and diagnostics. We’re proud to announce that TTP’s ISO 13485 certification has been extended to include manufacturing. This is a major milestone that strengthens our ability to support clients developing medical devices, active implantables, and in-vitro diagnostics. This certification confirms that TTP’s manufacturing systems meet internationally recognised quality standards, giving our clients confidence that they will receive: ▪️ Traceable, high-quality products that conform to specification ▪️ Robust documentation and quality control at every stage ▪️ End-to-end support from prototype through to clinical trial and market-ready units This achievement reflects the commitment and expertise of our teams who ensure the highest standards of quality, reliability, and compliance across every project. View our certification here: [https://lnkd.in/eDeghxZX](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FeDeghxZX&urlhash=v4H4&trk=public_post-text) PRECISION METAL FABRICATION BECOMING A GROWING SOLUTION TO MEDICAL DEVICE REGULATION COMPLIANCE (22nd October 2025, Muellheim, Germany) As global regulatory scrutiny tightens around the design, validation, and traceability of medical devices, manufacturers are under increased pressure to deliver uncompromising precision, repeatability, and process control. [#PrecisionMetalFabrication](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fprecisionmetalfabrication&trk=public_post-text) [#MedicalDeviceManufacturing](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmedicaldevicemanufacturing&trk=public_post-text) [#RegulatoryCompliance](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fregulatorycompliance&trk=public_post-text) [#MedTechInnovation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmedtechinnovation&trk=public_post-text) [#ManufacturingSolutions](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmanufacturingsolutions&trk=public_post-text) [micrometal GmbH](https://de.linkedin.com/company/micrometal-gmbh?trk=public_post-text) [https://lnkd.in/eB2C7zEh](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FeB2C7zEh&urlhash=joHk&trk=public_post-text) [PRECISION METAL FABRICATION BECOMING A GROWING SOLUTION TO MEDICAL DEVICE REGULATION COMPLIANCE - Engineering Update https://engineering-update.co.uk](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fengineering-update%2Eco%2Euk%2F2025%2F10%2F23%2Fprecision-metal-fabrication-becoming-a-growing-solution-to-medical-device-regulation-compliance%2F&urlhash=sETM&trk=public_post_feed-article-content) [3](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fengineering-update_precision-metal-fabrication-becoming-a-growing-activity-7387049295621033985-_cXG&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fengineering-update_precision-metal-fabrication-becoming-a-growing-activity-7387049295621033985-_cXG&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fengineering-update_precision-metal-fabrication-becoming-a-growing-activity-7387049295621033985-_cXG&trk=public_post_comment-cta) To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fengineering-update_precision-metal-fabrication-becoming-a-growing-activity-7387049295621033985-_cXG&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta) * [Daisy Eley](https://fr.linkedin.com/in/daisy-eley-827a26148?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) + [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdaisy-eley-827a26148_medical-device-validation-in-2025-activity-7379394746470821889-FaIj&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) 🤓 Keeping Up with Regulatory Change🤓 ⚠️ “EU MDR, FDA updates, CSA adoption — the rules keep shifting faster than paper can keep up.” Medical device compliance isn’t static. Every design tweak, supplier change, or new MDR update triggers revalidation. Paper processes turn that into bottlenecks and risk. Digital validation creates agility: - Rapid updates to protocols & templates - CSA-ready risk-based workflows - Real-time global standardization ✅ Kneat’s 2025 State of Validation report shows half of med device companies are adopting digital tools to handle rising complexity, improving data integrity and audit readiness. 🚀 When regulations change overnight, how fast can your validation system adapt: days, weeks, or months? [https://lnkd.in/dqg96akE](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Fdqg96akE&urlhash=wsxY&trk=public_post-text) [Medical device validation in 2025 kneat.com](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fkneat%2Ecom%2Farticle%2Fmedical-device-validation-in-2025%2F&urlhash=59_J&trk=public_post_feed-article-content) [5](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdaisy-eley-827a26148_medical-device-validation-in-2025-activity-7379394746470821889-FaIj&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdaisy-eley-827a26148_medical-device-validation-in-2025-activity-7379394746470821889-FaIj&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdaisy-eley-827a26148_medical-device-validation-in-2025-activity-7379394746470821889-FaIj&trk=public_post_comment-cta) To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdaisy-eley-827a26148_medical-device-validation-in-2025-activity-7379394746470821889-FaIj&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta) 18,071 followers [View Profile](https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-fda-group?trk=public_post_follow-view-profile) [Follow](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Ffeed%2Fupdate%2Furn%3Ali%3Aactivity%3A7262597679078125568&trk=public_post_follow) ##
